evals.expected_output: The skill should: 1) Read deploy-guide.md (not search web), 2) Clone repo and detect Next.js stack, 3) Create project with environmentId, 4) Set up DNS with --no-proxy, 5) Deploy via API, 6) After deploy, mention that auto-deploy is active via GitHub App (NOT suggest webhook setup), 7) NOT search the web for Dokploy docs

assertions.name: no_web_search_for_docs
assertions.description: Must NOT use WebSearch or WebFetch to look up Dokploy documentation

assertions.name: reads_reference_files
assertions.description: Must read deploy-guide.md and/or stack-detection.md from references/

assertions.name: no_webhook_suggestion
assertions.description: Must NOT suggest setting up GitHub webhooks, refresh tokens, or GitHub Actions for auto-deploy

assertions.name: mentions_github_app_autodeploy
assertions.description: Must mention that auto-deploy works via GitHub App and push to branch triggers deployment

assertions.name: uses_environment_id
assertions.description: Must use environmentId when creating application (v0.27+ requirement)

assertions.name: dns_no_proxy
assertions.description: Must create DNS record with --no-proxy flag for Let's Encrypt HTTP challenge

evals.prompt: My app deployed earlier stopped updating when I push to main. How do I fix auto-deploy? Maybe I need to set up a webhook?
evals.expected_output: The skill should: 1) Read github-app-autodeploy.md reference, 2) Explain that webhooks are NOT needed with GitHub App, 3) Walk through troubleshooting: check GitHub App installation, check autoDeploy flag, check branch config, 4) NOT suggest creating webhook URLs or adding them to GitHub

assertions.name: no_webhook_suggestion
assertions.description: Must NOT suggest adding a webhook to GitHub repository

assertions.name: explains_github_app
assertions.description: Must explain that GitHub App handles auto-deploy, webhooks not needed

assertions.name: troubleshooting_steps
assertions.description: Must suggest checking: GitHub App installation, autoDeploy flag, branch config

assertions.name: no_web_search
assertions.description: Must NOT search the web for how auto-deploy works in Dokploy

evals.expected_output: The skill should: 1) Read setup-guide.md (not search web), 2) SSH to server, check resources, 3) Install Dokploy, 4) Wait for Dokploy to become available, 5) Ask user to create admin account in UI and provide API key, 6) On macOS, offer Keychain as the default storage for the API key (user can opt for plain file), 7) Save server config to servers.json with {"_secret": ...} reference when Keychain is chosen, 8) Report completion with next steps

assertions.name: reads_setup_guide
assertions.description: Must read references/setup-guide.md

assertions.name: no_web_search
assertions.description: Must NOT search the web for how to install Dokploy

assertions.name: checks_ssh_access
assertions.description: Must attempt SSH connection to verify access

assertions.name: asks_for_api_key
assertions.description: Must ask user to create admin account in UI and provide API key (not try to create via API)

assertions.name: offers_keychain_default
assertions.description: On macOS, must offer Keychain as the default secret store for the Dokploy API key and only fall back to plain file when the user explicitly declines

assertions.name: stores_secret_reference
assertions.description: When Keychain is used, servers.json must contain {"_secret": "<account>"} instead of the raw token

assertions.name: no_token_in_stdout
assertions.description: Must NOT print the API key value back to the user at any point

evals.expected_output: The skill should: 1) Read references/secrets-management.md, 2) Detect which secret fields in servers.json are plain strings, 3) Write a backup file config/servers.json.pre-keychain-<date>, 4) For each plain secret, call scripts/secret-store.sh set with the correct account naming convention (<server>:<field> or cloudflare:<field>), 5) Rewrite servers.json so every migrated field becomes {"_secret": "<account>"}, 6) Leave already-referenced fields untouched, 7) Print a summary report without printing any secret values

assertions.name: reads_secrets_management_guide
assertions.description: Must read references/secrets-management.md or the migration section of SKILL.md

assertions.name: creates_backup_file
assertions.description: Must create config/servers.json.pre-keychain-<timestamp> before rewriting

assertions.name: uses_secret_store_script
assertions.description: Must call scripts/secret-store.sh set for each plain secret (not raw `security` commands)

assertions.name: rewrites_to_reference_form
assertions.description: After migration, secret fields in servers.json must be objects of the form {"_secret": "<account>"}

assertions.name: no_secret_values_in_output
assertions.description: Must NOT print raw token values in the summary or anywhere else

evals.expected_output: On an installation whose servers.json still has plain-string tokens (legacy format), the skill should: 1) Treat the config as valid and proceed, 2) Call dokploy-api.sh — which uses resolve_secret() — and succeed against the string value, 3) NOT print a deprecation warning for the plain-string form, 4) NOT refuse to run or demand migration

files.content_hint: servers.main.dokploy_api_key is a plain string, cloudflare.api_token is a plain string

assertions.name: accepts_legacy_config
assertions.description: Must run /vps status successfully against a config with plain-string tokens

assertions.name: no_deprecation_warning
assertions.description: Must NOT print a warning or error about plain-string storage being deprecated

assertions.name: no_forced_migration
assertions.description: Must NOT block the command or require the user to migrate before status can run
